prose:
Method-provenance summary (frontier orbitals of furan)
Property: HOMO and LUMO one-electron energies and HOMO–LUMO gap (eV)
Level of theory: DFT, B3LYP/def2-TZVP (no density-fitting; full four-center RKS) in PySCF (version not reported)
Geometry provenance: user-supplied /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/furan/furan.xyz; single-point only (no re-optimization) ⇒ DFT//input-XYZ
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvent)
Charge = 0, multiplicity = 1 (closed-shell singlet)
SCF convergence: converged in 9 cycles; integrity.trustworthy = true; no warnings reported.
Live run log was streamed to the working directory (frontier_<timestamp>.out) while the calculation executed; result JSON saved as furan_frontier_dft.json (see cli_invocation path in JSON for exact location).

Key results (eV):
• HOMO = −6.3597638
• LUMO = −0.1024036
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.2573602 eV (Koopmans-type orbital gap, not an excitation energy)

These values were obtained with the chemkit "frontier-orbitals" skill calling PySCF at the specified B3LYP/def2-TZVP level in the gas phase.

No engine warnings were reported.
